aiProductPages.note: All ~34 KB each — consistent and reasonable

recommendations.suggestion: Investigate why this page is 637 KB. Likely inline data blobs or large component trees. Consider code-splitting, lazy loading sections, or paginating content.
This comparison page likely embeds all service data inline. Consider fetching from an API endpoint instead of inlining JSON blobs.
Homepage is large. Check for unused JS bundles, consider dynamic imports for below-fold content, and review third-party scripts.
Likely has inline service data. Consider server-side data fetching and component lazy loading.
Manageable size but could paginate or virtualize blog list for larger post counts.

imagesAndAltText.finding: No raw tags found in any .tsx file across the entire project. The project uses Next.js without next/image imports, relying instead on CSS backgrounds, SVG-like divs, and emoji characters for visual elements. No missing alt text issues.

nextImageUsage.note: No next/image imports detected — all visual elements use CSS/styling or emoji

emojiAccessibility.note: 67 occurrences of aria-hidden='true' used properly on decorative emoji icons, checkmarks, and background decorations. Emojis in text content (headings, button labels) are left visible since they convey meaning as text.

headingHierarchy.finding: All scanned pages have proper <h1> tags. Heading hierarchy generally follows h1 → h2 → h3 patterns. No pages found without an h1.
headingHierarchy.headingStructure: Good — every route page includes a descriptive h1 tag styled appropriately. Blog posts use h1 for the article title. Category landing pages use h1 for the page heading. Sub-sections use h2/h3 correctly.

ariaLabels.finding: 36 aria-label attributes found across app components providing meaningful labels for interactive elements

skipToContent.finding: Skip-to-content link present in AccessibilityEnhancer.tsx wrapping the entire app via app/layout.tsx. Targets '#main-content' div.
skipToContent.implementation: 'use client' component with mount detection for proper SSR handling

keyboardNavigation.finding: Keyboard navigation helper script injected via AccessibilityEnhancer. Adds .keyboard-nav class to body when Tab key is pressed (supports visible focus styles), removes on mouse click.
keyboardNavigation.note: No custom tabIndex found — default tab order is used, which is generally preferred

langAttribute.finding: Root <html> tag has lang='en' set in app/layout.tsx

structuredData.finding: JSON-LD structured data injected in root layout for Organization and Website schemas. Additional structured data components available (LocalBusinessStructuredData.tsx, ArticleStructuredData.tsx, BlogPostStructuredData.tsx)

colorContrast.finding: Manual color contrast verification not performed in this automated scan. Site uses dark theme with white text (rgb(255,255,255)) on dark backgrounds (slate-950 ~rgb(2,6,23)), which provides high contrast by default. Verify interactive states and component-specific color combinations separately.
colorContrast.note: A dedicated WCAG Color Contrast Checker tool exists at /tools/color-contrast-checker/ (returns 404 on live site — may need route exposure)

keyIssues.issue: industry-solutions page is 637 KB — likely the heaviest single page load
service-comparison page is 469 KB — inline service data bloat
Homepage is 271 KB — should target under 200 KB for initial load
ai-services page is 249 KB — inline data optimization needed

strengths: Excellent aria-label coverage across navigation, search, social links, breadcrumbs, and interactive widgets
Skip-to-content link available on all pages
Keyboard navigation detection with visible focus style support
Proper lang='en' and structured data present
No missing alt text issues (no raw tags used)
All pages have proper h1 tags with consistent heading hierarchy
Decorative elements properly hidden from screen readers with aria-hidden
Blog posts are consistently sized at ~32 KB — well optimized
quickWins: Investigate inline data blobs in /industry-solutions/ and /service-comparison/ — likely the highest ROI performance fix
Consider code-splitting homepage hero section from below-fold content to reduce initial payload
Review ai-services page for inline data that could be fetched server-side

scanNotes: Scanned all .tsx files across app/, tools/, src/, and root directories. WCAG checks focused on img alt text, heading hierarchy, aria-labels, and skip-to-content. Color contrast was NOT tested programmatically. Performance measured via curl (transfer size, not rendered size).
